steps.title: Use similar triangles
steps.content: Since $\overline{LM}\parallel\overline{PQ}$, the two triangles are similar. The corresponding side lengths $MR=8$ and $RP=14$ give a linear scale factor from $\triangle LMR$ to $\triangle PQR$ of $$\frac{14}{8}=\frac{7}{4}$$

steps.title: Scale the area
steps.content: Areas scale by the square of the linear scale factor: $$36\left(\frac{7}{4}\right)^2=36\cdot\frac{49}{16}=\frac{441}{4}$$

steps.title: Confirm the choice
steps.content: The correct answer is $\frac{441}{4}$, so D is correct.
